data.author: clalancette
data.message:
Change the not_composable example to destroy subscription first. (#210)

* Change the not_composable example to not use a global node.

We are seeing the following message when hitting Ctrl-C
in the subscriber not_composable node:

[ERROR] [rclcpp]: Error in destruction of rcl subscription handle: the Node Handle was destructed too early. You will leak memory

The problem is that setting g_node to nullptr causes the
node destructor to be run before the subscriber is destructed.
Due to another bug, we can't just remove that assignment, so
instead we make it all locally scoped in main for now, which
works around the problem.

Signed-off-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@openrobotics.org>

* Manually destroy the subscription first.

Signed-off-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@openrobotics.org>

data.author: vmayoral
data.message:
Intraprocess prototype

ROS 2 publisher and subscriber exchange a ROS message (string
in this example) through a pointer to the data. This is useful
for intraprocess comunication where instead of transferring all
the data through the underlying dds, just a pointer (uint_64) is
transferred.

The implementation uses a struct that keeps a pointer to the data
and a count.
